steps.title: Identify the line x = 2
steps.content: The equation $x = 2$ represents all points with x-coordinate 2, regardless of y. This is a vertical line.

steps.title: Apply the perpendicular relationship
steps.content:
A line perpendicular to a vertical line must be a horizontal line. Horizontal lines have constant y-values and no vertical change:
$$m = \frac{\text{rise}}{\text{run}} = \frac{0}{\text{any nonzero run}} = 0$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
